Two-Track Cost Analysis
ESOP vs Traditional M&A — Comprehensive Cost Comparison
Company
{{companyName}}
Seller
{{sellerName}}
Date
{{generatedDate}}
Reference
{{ref}}
§ 01Executive Summary

This analysis compares the total cost of an ESOP transaction (Track A) against a traditional M&A sale (Track B) over a five-year horizon. Both tracks are modeled on the same enterprise value. The ESOP track includes one-time transaction costs and ongoing annual administration. The M&A track includes broker fees, legal, accounting, and due diligence costs typical of a middle-market sale.

Key Finding: The ESOP path delivers estimated savings of {{savings}} over five years compared to traditional M&A, driven primarily by the elimination of the sell-side broker fee and favorable tax treatment under IRC §1042.
§ 02Track A: ESOP Transaction Costs
Track A — ESOP

The ESOP track includes transaction setup costs incurred at closing, plus ongoing annual plan administration and compliance costs.

Cost ItemAmount
Forhemit stewardship fee{{esopTransactionCosts}}
Independent trustee engagement{{esopTrusteeFee}}
ERISA counsel{{esopErisaFee}}
Valuation / FMV appraisal{{esopValuationFee}}
QofE / financial due diligence{{esopQofeFee}}
Plan document & TPA setup{{esopPlanDocFee}}
SBA loan packaging & closing{{esopSbaFee}}
Total One-Time Costs{{esopOneTimeTotal}}
Annual Cost ItemPer Year
Plan administration & TPA{{esopAnnualAdmin}}
Annual valuation update{{esopAnnualValuation}}
ERISA compliance & audit{{esopAnnualCompliance}}
Total Annual Costs{{esopAnnualCosts}}
Five-Year ESOP TotalAmount
One-time transaction costs{{esopOneTimeTotal}}
Annual costs × 5 years{{esopFiveYearAnnual}}
ESOP Total (5-Year){{esopFiveYearTotal}}
§ 03Track B: Traditional M&A Costs
Track B — Traditional M&A

The traditional M&A track includes sell-side advisory, legal, accounting, and due diligence costs. Broker fees in middle-market transactions typically range from 4% to 8% of enterprise value, with additional professional fees layered on top.

Cost ComponentAmount
Sell-side broker / M&A advisor fee{{maBrokerFee}}
Seller's legal counsel{{maLegalCosts}}
Accounting & tax advisory{{maAccountingCosts}}
Buyer-side due diligence (seller cost-share){{maDdCosts}}
Data room & transaction management{{maDataRoomCosts}}
Representation & warranty insurance{{maRwiCosts}}
M&A Total (at Close){{maTotal}}
Note: Traditional M&A costs are substantially incurred at closing. There are minimal ongoing costs post-close unless an earnout or escrow arrangement creates additional advisory needs.
§ 04Net Cost Comparison (5-Year)
MetricTrack A: ESOPTrack B: M&A
Transaction costs (at close){{esopOneTimeTotal}}{{maTotal}}
Ongoing costs (5-year){{esopFiveYearAnnual}}$0
Total Cost (5-Year){{esopFiveYearTotal}}{{maTotal}}
Difference{{savings}} savings via ESOP
§ 05Tax-Adjusted Comparison

The cost comparison changes materially when tax effects are considered. ESOP transaction costs are deductible as ordinary business expenses. Under IRC §1042, the seller may defer capital gains tax on the sale proceeds, producing a significant after-tax advantage.

After-Tax MetricTrack A: ESOPTrack B: M&A
Total transaction + ongoing costs{{esopFiveYearTotal}}{{maTotal}}
Corporate tax shield (cost deduction){{esopTaxShield}}{{maTaxShield}}
Seller capital gains deferral (§1042){{sellerTaxDeferral}}$0
Tax-Adjusted Total Cost{{taxAdjustedESOP}}{{taxAdjustedMA}}
Tax-Adjusted Savings{{savings}}
Important: Tax-adjusted figures are illustrative and depend on entity type, seller basis, state of incorporation, and applicable federal and state tax rates. The §1042 deferral requires reinvestment in Qualified Replacement Securities. Consult your CPA and tax counsel for personalized analysis.
§ 06Qualitative Considerations
1.Employee retention. ESOPs create employee ownership culture, reducing turnover and improving productivity — costs not captured above.
2.Legacy preservation. An ESOP maintains the company's name, culture, and community presence. M&A often results in rebranding or consolidation.
3.Timeline control. The ESOP process is structured and predictable (120 days). M&A timelines are variable and subject to buyer-driven delays.
4.Continuity of management. ESOP sellers can continue running the company post-close. M&A typically requires transition or departure.
5.Deal certainty. ESOP transactions have a known buyer (the trust) and a structured financing path. M&A deals face buyer due diligence renegotiation risk.
§ 07Next Steps

To refine these estimates with actual figures for {{companyName}}:

1.QofE engagement. Engage a quality-of-earnings provider to normalize TTM EBITDA. This drives enterprise value on both tracks.
2.Entity structure review. Confirm entity type (C-corp, S-corp, LLC) and any conversion requirements for §1042 eligibility.
3.Tax modeling. Your CPA should run personalized tax scenarios based on your basis, filing status, and state of residence.
